commands.title: Read sanitized config
commands.summary: Read sanitized configuration facts.
commands.description:
Returns sanitized configuration facts: the open target, whether this open created the database, durability, and the default branch. Only allowlisted, non-sensitive facts are exposed; no filesystem paths, credentials, or provider keys are ever returned.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_admin_config
mcp.description: Use this when the user asks how the database is configured — its open target, durability, and default branch.

commands.title: Read one config value
commands.summary: Read one sanitized configuration value by key.
commands.description:
Returns one sanitized configuration value from the allowlist by key, or a null value when the key is not recognized. Only allowlisted, non-sensitive keys are served; an empty key is rejected with `invalid_argument.engine.config_key`.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_admin_config_key
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants a single configuration value by key rather than the whole sanitized config.

commands.title: Describe database
commands.summary: Read a compact description of the database.
commands.description:
Returns a compact description of the database for one branch: engine version, open target, the default and described branches, all active branches, the registered product spaces, per-primitive counts (KV, JSON, event, plus vector-collection and graph summaries), the sanitized config, and the available capabilities. The branch defaults to the handle branch when omitted.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_admin_describe
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants an overview of a database — its branches, spaces, primitive counts, and available capabilities.

commands.title: Read database health
commands.summary: Read control-plane health facts.
commands.description:
Returns control-plane health facts: the worst overall status plus per-subsystem status for identity, registry, branch catalog, and the optional branch-local space catalog. Also reports the default branch and active branch count. A healthy result means every required control-plane fact is present and readable.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_admin_health
mcp.description: Use this when the user asks whether the database is healthy or wants to diagnose a degraded or unavailable control plane.

commands.title: Clone hub dataset
commands.summary: Clone a dataset from a hub into a new local database.
commands.description:
Clones a dataset from a hub into a new local database directory. Resolution, download, verification, reconstitution, and origin recording run once behind this command; the session database is not touched. The destination directory must not exist or must be empty. When the hub URL is not given, the layered resolver selects it from the flag, environment, and config layers.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_admin_hub_clone
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to download or clone a published hub dataset into a new local database directory.

fixtures.replay_skip: hub clone resolves and downloads a dataset over the network into a new database directory; it never runs against the scratch cache executor

commands.title: Read database info
commands.summary: Read database identity and a catalog summary.
commands.description:
Returns database identity and a catalog summary for one branch: engine version, open target, whether this open created the database, durability, the default branch, the active branch count, and the registered space count for the selected branch. The branch defaults to the handle branch when omitted.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_admin_info
mcp.description: Use this when the user asks what database this is, whether it was just created, or how many branches and spaces it holds.

commands.title: Read database metrics
commands.summary: Read lightweight database metrics.
commands.description:
Returns lightweight database metrics: the open target, durability, whether the handle is open, the active branch count, the registered space count for the selected branch, and the control-plane health status. The branch defaults to the handle branch when omitted.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_admin_metrics
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants quick database metrics such as branch and space counts and the control-plane status.

commands.title: Ping database
commands.summary: Check that the database handle is live.
commands.description:
Lightweight liveness check. Returns the engine package version without touching branches, spaces, or primitive data. Use it to confirm the handle is open and responsive before issuing heavier commands.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_admin_ping
mcp.description: Use this for a fast liveness probe that confirms the database responds and reports its engine version.

commands.title: Read remote origin
commands.summary: Read where this database was cloned from.
commands.description:
Reads the remote origin recorded when this database was cloned from a hub: the remote URL, dataset, branch, manifest hash, fetch time, and per-branch base frontier. Returns a null origin when the database was created locally and never cloned.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_admin_remote
mcp.description: Use this when the user asks where a database came from or whether it was cloned from a hub dataset.

commands.title: Export Arrow file
commands.summary: Export a product primitive to an Arrow-compatible file.
commands.description:
Exports a product primitive from the selected branch and space to an Arrow-compatible file (Parquet, CSV, or JSONL). Graph exports treat the path as a stem and write separate node and edge files. Returns a summary of the exported primitive, the concrete output paths, the row count, and the total output size.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_arrow_export
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to write KV, JSON, event, vector, or graph data out to a Parquet, CSV, or JSONL file.

fixtures.replay_skip: arrow export writes real files to a filesystem path outside the scratch cache executor

commands.title: Import Arrow file
commands.summary: Import an Arrow-compatible file into a product primitive.
commands.description:
Imports an Arrow-compatible file (Parquet, CSV, or JSONL) into a product primitive on the selected branch and space. Rows are written through the standard batch commands, so the import commits like any other write. Returns a summary of the target primitive, the input file, and the imported, skipped, and batch counts.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_arrow_import
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to load a Parquet, CSV, or JSONL file into KV, JSON, or a vector collection.

fixtures.replay_skip: arrow import reads a real file from the filesystem; the source file does not exist on the scratch cache executor

commands.title: Create empty branch
commands.summary: Create a new empty root branch.
commands.description:
Creates an empty root branch with no parent and no data. This is not a fork: the new branch starts from nothing, and its `parent` is null. Use `branch.fork` to start from an existing branch's data. Creating a name that already exists fails with `already_exists.engine.branch`; names reserved for engine control data are rejected.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_branch_create
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants a fresh branch that shares no history with any existing branch.

commands.title: Delete branch
commands.summary: Delete an active branch and release its storage claims.
commands.description:
Deletes an active branch and reports the deleted branch summary, generation facts, and storage cleanup counts. The `default` branch refuses deletion with `invalid_argument.engine.branch_delete`. There is no merge in V1: work on a fork is either kept by continuing on that branch or discarded by deleting it.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_branch_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove a branch. The default branch cannot be deleted.

commands.title: Fork branch from current head
commands.summary: Fork a new branch from the current head of a source branch.
commands.description:
Forks a new branch from the source branch's current head. The new branch sees all data visible on the source at fork time; later writes on either branch stay isolated. The returned branch summary records the parent name, fork version, and generation.

On the CLI, all three fork commands share the single verb `strata branch fork <SOURCE> <BRANCH>`: with no flags it runs this command, while `--version` routes to `branch.fork_at_version` and `--timestamp` routes to `branch.fork_at_timestamp` (both wire-surface commands).

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_branch_fork
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to branch off the latest state of an existing branch.

commands.title: Fork branch at timestamp
commands.summary: Fork a new branch from a retained source timestamp.
commands.description:
Forks a new branch anchored at a retained source timestamp (microseconds, on Strata's logical commit clock). The engine resolves the timestamp to the covering retained commit; the returned parent lineage records both the fork timestamp and the resolved fork version. A timestamp outside retained history fails with `history_unavailable.engine.persistence_history`.

This command has no dedicated CLI verb: the CLI expresses it as `strata branch fork <SOURCE> <BRANCH> --timestamp <TIMESTAMP>` (one shared `branch fork` verb routes to all three fork commands, so only `branch.fork` owns the CLI path). It remains fully reachable through the generic wire surface — `strata command run`, MCP, and SDKs.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_branch_fork_at_timestamp
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to branch from an existing branch as of a point in time.

commands.title: Fork branch at version
commands.summary: Fork a new branch from a retained source commit version.
commands.description:
Forks a new branch anchored at a retained commit version of the source branch, giving time-travel semantics: the new branch sees exactly the data visible at that version. A version outside retained history fails with `history_unavailable.engine.persistence_history`.

This command has no dedicated CLI verb: the CLI expresses it as `strata branch fork <SOURCE> <BRANCH> --version <VERSION>` (one shared `branch fork` verb routes to all three fork commands, so only `branch.fork` owns the CLI path). It remains fully reachable through the generic wire surface — `strata command run`, MCP, and SDKs.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_branch_fork_at_version
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to branch from a specific historical commit version of an existing branch.

commands.title: Read one branch
commands.summary: Read one branch summary by name.
commands.description:
Reads the summary for one branch: name, deterministic branch id, generation, status, parent lineage, and logical creation version. A branch that does not exist is a `not_found.engine.branch` error, not an empty result.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_branch_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user asks about a specific branch's status, lineage, or fork point.

commands.title: List branches
commands.summary: List active branches with their lineage facts.
commands.description:
Lists every active branch as a terminal page. Each item carries the branch name, deterministic branch id, generation, status, parent lineage (fork version and timestamp when forked), and logical creation version.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_branch_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to see all branches in the database.

commands.title: Append event
commands.summary: Append one event to the branch event log.
commands.description:
Appends one event to the selected branch and space. Strata assigns the next sequence number, stamps the event with its append timestamp, and links it into the tamper-evident hash chain. Events are immutable once appended.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_event_append
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to record, log, or emit a single application event with a type and JSON payload.

commands.title: Batch append events
commands.summary: Append multiple events in one commit.
commands.description:
Appends multiple events to the selected branch and space in one engine commit. Sequences are assigned in entry order. Entries that fail validation report a positional item error while valid entries still append.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_event_batch_append
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to record several events atomically in a single engine commit.

commands.title: Count events
commands.summary: Count visible events in the log.
commands.description:
Counts events visible in the selected branch and space. The optional timestamp counts the events visible at that commit time.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_event_count
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants the number of events in the event log, optionally at a historical point in time.

commands.title: Check event existence
commands.summary: Check whether an event sequence exists.
commands.description:
Checks whether one event sequence exists in the selected branch and space without returning the record.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_event_exists
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to test whether a specific event sequence number is present without reading the record.

commands.title: Get event
commands.summary: Read one event by sequence number.
commands.description:
Reads one event from the selected branch and space by sequence number. The optional timestamp reads the record visible at that commit time. The record carries its payload, append timestamp, and hash-chain facts.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_event_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to fetch a single event record by its sequence number.

fixtures.replay_skip: wall-clock event timestamps are hashed into event content; outputs never reproduce (see engine next_event_timestamp)

commands.title: List events
commands.summary: List events with optional type filter and cursor.
commands.description:
Lists events from the selected branch and space in sequence order. An optional event type narrows the results, the sequence cursor continues a previous page, and the optional timestamp reads the log visible at that commit time.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_event_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to page through events, optionally filtered by event type, using a sequence cursor.

fixtures.replay_skip: wall-clock event timestamps are hashed into event content; outputs never reproduce (see engine next_event_timestamp)

commands.title: Read event sequence range
commands.summary: Read a range of events by sequence number.
commands.description:
Reads events from the selected branch and space by sequence range. The start sequence is inclusive and the optional end sequence is exclusive; reverse direction walks backward from the start sequence. An optional event type narrows the results.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_event_range
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants events between two sequence numbers, optionally filtered by event type, in forward or reverse order.

fixtures.replay_skip: wall-clock event timestamps are hashed into event content; outputs never reproduce (see engine next_event_timestamp)

commands.title: Read event time range
commands.summary: Read a range of events by occurrence time.
commands.description:
Reads events from the selected branch and space whose append timestamps fall inside an inclusive microsecond window. This queries when events occurred; historical log states are the timestamped read commands' job. An optional event type narrows the results.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_event_range_time
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants events whose append timestamps fall inside a time window, optionally filtered by event type.

fixtures.replay_skip: wall-clock event timestamps are hashed into event content; outputs never reproduce (see engine next_event_timestamp)

commands.title: List event types
commands.summary: List distinct event types in the log.
commands.description:
Lists the distinct event types visible in the selected branch and space in sorted order. The optional timestamp lists the types visible at that commit time.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_event_types
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants the set of event types recorded in the event log.

commands.title: Verify event chain
commands.summary: Verify event log density and hash linkage.
commands.description:
Verifies that the visible event log in the selected branch and space is dense and hash-linked: sequences are contiguous from zero, the genesis record links to the all-zeros hash, and every record's hash matches its content and predecessor.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_event_verify_chain
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to audit the event log's integrity by checking sequence density and the hash chain.

commands.title: Traverse graph breadth-first
commands.summary: Run a bounded breadth-first traversal.
commands.description:
Runs a breadth-first traversal from a start node over a consistent snapshot, bounded by `max_depth` (default 100) and `max_nodes` (default 10000). Returns visited node ids in traversal order, a depth per node, and the tree edges in discovery order. Direction defaults to `outgoing`; an optional edge-type list restricts every hop. The start node must exist (`not_found.engine.graph_node`).

Analytics commands compute over a consistent snapshot of the visible graph and return a complete result payload in one response. They accept optional snapshot budgets and an `as_of` timestamp for time travel; results are deterministic for a fixed graph state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_analytics_bfs
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to explore outward from a node level by level - reachability, hop distance, or a bounded expansion with depth, node, and edge-type limits.

commands.title: Detect graph communities
commands.summary: Detect communities via label propagation.
commands.description:
Detects communities by label propagation over a consistent snapshot: every node repeatedly adopts the most common label among its neighbors until labels stabilize or the iteration bound (default 10) is reached. Every node maps to its community representative node id. Propagation direction defaults to `both`. Accepts an optional snapshot budget and `as_of` for time travel.

Analytics commands compute over a consistent snapshot of the visible graph and return a complete result payload in one response. They accept optional snapshot budgets and an `as_of` timestamp for time travel; results are deterministic for a fixed graph state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_analytics_cdlp
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants community detection, graph clustering, or group structure via the community detection label propagation (CDLP) algorithm.

commands.title: Compute graph clustering coefficients
commands.summary: Compute local clustering coefficients.
commands.description:
Computes the local clustering coefficient for every node over a consistent snapshot: the fraction of a node's neighbor pairs that are themselves connected. Nodes in fully-triangulated neighborhoods score 1.0. Accepts an optional snapshot budget and `as_of` for time travel.

Analytics commands compute over a consistent snapshot of the visible graph and return a complete result payload in one response. They accept optional snapshot budgets and an `as_of` timestamp for time travel; results are deterministic for a fixed graph state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_analytics_lcc
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants per-node clustering coefficients - how densely each node's neighbors connect to each other (triangle density).

commands.title: Compute graph pagerank
commands.summary: Compute PageRank importance scores.
commands.description:
Computes PageRank over a consistent snapshot. Tunable damping (default 0.85), iteration bound (default 20), and convergence tolerance (default 1e-6); the response reports how many iterations actually ran. Optional personalization seeds steer both teleport and dangling mass toward weighted nodes, and the response flags `personalized: true`. Results are deterministic for a fixed graph state. Accepts an optional snapshot budget and `as_of` for time travel.

Analytics commands compute over a consistent snapshot of the visible graph and return a complete result payload in one response. They accept optional snapshot budgets and an `as_of` timestamp for time travel; results are deterministic for a fixed graph state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_analytics_pagerank
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants node importance, influence ranking, or personalized PageRank with seed weights.

commands.title: Compute graph shortest paths
commands.summary: Compute shortest-path distances from a source.
commands.description:
Computes weighted shortest-path distances from a source node over a consistent snapshot. Edge weights (default 1.0) accumulate along paths; unreachable nodes are omitted from the result. Direction defaults to `outgoing`. The source node must exist (`not_found.engine.graph_node`). Accepts an optional snapshot budget and `as_of` for time travel.

Analytics commands compute over a consistent snapshot of the visible graph and return a complete result payload in one response. They accept optional snapshot budgets and an `as_of` timestamp for time travel; results are deterministic for a fixed graph state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_analytics_sssp
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants weighted shortest-path distances from one node to every reachable node (single-source shortest path / Dijkstra-style queries).

commands.title: Compute graph connected components
commands.summary: Compute weakly connected components.
commands.description:
Computes weakly connected components over a consistent snapshot of the graph, ignoring edge direction. Every node maps to its component representative - the smallest node id in its component - and the response carries the distinct component count. Accepts an optional snapshot budget and `as_of` for time travel.

Analytics commands compute over a consistent snapshot of the visible graph and return a complete result payload in one response. They accept optional snapshot budgets and an `as_of` timestamp for time travel; results are deterministic for a fixed graph state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_analytics_wcc
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to find connected components, clusters of mutually reachable nodes, or check whether a graph is fully connected. Reachable via the generic command runner or the CLI verb `graph wcc`.

commands.title: Apply graph delete policy
commands.summary: Apply a delete policy to bound graph facts.
commands.description:
Applies an explicit policy to every graph node bound to the given entity target: `cascade` deletes the bound nodes and their incident edges, `detach` keeps the nodes but removes their bindings, and `keep_dangling` preserves the bindings so traversal can report the target's status. The acknowledgement reports how many bound nodes the policy covered.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_apply_delete_policy
mcp.description: Use this after deleting an entity in another primitive, when the user must decide what happens to graph nodes bound to it - cascade-delete them, detach the bindings, or keep them dangling. Wire-only; invoke via the generic command runner.

commands.title: Batch write graph
commands.summary: Apply graph mutations atomically.
commands.description:
Applies a list of graph operations - `upsert_node`, `delete_node`, `upsert_edge`, `delete_edge` - in one engine commit. Validation failures (bad ids, missing edge endpoints, frozen-ontology violations) reject the whole batch; nothing is partially applied. The response reports one positional item result per operation, all sharing the same commit receipt.

Atomic batches validate every operation up front and apply all of them in one engine commit, or none at all. The response still reports one positional item result per operation; all item results share the same commit receipt.

mcp.name: strata_graph_batch_write
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants several graph mutations (node/edge upserts and deletes) applied together in one atomic commit - all succeed or none do. Wire-only; invoke via the generic command runner.

commands.title: List graph bindings for entity
commands.summary: Find graph nodes bound to an entity.
commands.description:
Searches every graph in the selected branch and space for nodes whose entity binding matches the given target (primitive, space, key). This is the reverse index of node bindings: given an entity, find the graph facts attached to it. Results paginate by an opaque cursor.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_graph_bindings
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to find every graph node bound to a specific KV, JSON, vector, event, or graph entity (reverse lookup from entity to nodes). Wire-only; invoke via the generic command runner.

commands.title: Bulk insert graph data
commands.summary: Bulk-load nodes and edges in chunks.
commands.description:
Ingests a payload of nodes and edges in chunked commits: nodes first, then edges, so edges may reference nodes from the same payload. Node objects use the key `node_id`; edges use `src`, `edge_type`, `dst`, and optional `weight` (default 1.0) and `properties`. `chunk_size` bounds items per commit (default 512, clamped at 800). The acknowledgement reports inserted counts, the number of chunk commits, and the final chunk's commit receipt.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_bulk_insert
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to load many nodes and edges into a graph at once. Nodes commit before edges in chunked commits; edge endpoints must exist or arrive in the same payload.

commands.title: Create graph
commands.summary: Create a named graph.
commands.description:
Creates an empty named graph in the selected space and returns its metadata, including node and edge counts (zero at creation) and the create commit coordinates. A database can hold many graphs; graph names are unique per branch and space. Creating a name that already exists fails with `already_exists.engine.graph`.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_create
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to create a new named graph for nodes and edges. Fails if a graph with this name already exists.

commands.title: Delete graph
commands.summary: Delete a graph and its visible data.
commands.description:
Deletes a named graph and every visible node, edge, binding, and ontology row it owns. Deleting a graph that does not exist is not an error: the acknowledgement reports `deleted: false` with a `not_found` effect. Earlier states remain readable through time travel on other commands.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to delete an entire named graph, including all of its nodes and edges. Returns deleted=false if the graph does not exist.

commands.title: Add graph edge
commands.summary: Add or replace a graph edge.
commands.description:
Adds a directed edge `src -[edge_type]-> dst` or replaces it if the same triple already exists. Both endpoints must already exist; writing an edge to a missing node fails with `invalid_argument.engine.graph_edge_endpoint`. Weight defaults to 1.0 and must not be negative. Once the graph's ontology is frozen, the edge type and its endpoint object types are validated against the declared link types.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_edge_add
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to connect two existing nodes with a directed, typed edge, optionally weighted or carrying JSON properties.

commands.title: Get graph edge
commands.summary: Read one graph edge.
commands.description:
Reads one edge by its `(src, edge_type, dst)` triple, returning its weight, properties, and last-write commit coordinates. A missing edge reads back as no data. Accepts `as_of` for time travel.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_graph_edge_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to read an edge's weight or properties by its source, edge type, and destination. Returns null for a missing edge.

commands.title: Remove graph edge
commands.summary: Remove a graph edge.
commands.description:
Removes one directed edge by its `(src, edge_type, dst)` triple. The endpoints are untouched. Removing an edge that does not exist is not an error: the acknowledgement reports `deleted: false`.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_edge_remove
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to delete one directed edge identified by source, edge type, and destination.

commands.title: List graphs
commands.summary: List graph names.
commands.description:
Lists graph names in lexicographic order. Accepts an optional item limit (default 100), an exclusive name cursor for continuation, and an `as_of` timestamp to list the graphs visible at an earlier instant.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_graph_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to see which named graphs exist in the current branch and space.

commands.title: Read graph metadata
commands.summary: Read graph metadata and counts.
commands.description:
Reads a graph's metadata: live node and edge counts plus the create and last-update commit versions and timestamps. Reading a graph that does not exist returns no data rather than an error. Accepts `as_of` for time travel.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_graph_meta
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants a graph's node count, edge count, or creation and update commit coordinates. Returns null if the graph does not exist.

commands.title: List graph neighbors
commands.summary: List a node's neighbors.
commands.description:
Walks a node's edges and returns one hit per traversed edge. Direction is `outgoing`, `incoming`, or `both`; an optional edge-type filter restricts the walk. Each hit embeds both the traversed edge and the neighbor node in full, so a follow-up read is rarely needed. A missing node yields an empty page.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_graph_neighbors
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants the nodes adjacent to a given node - who it points to, who points to it, or both - optionally filtered by edge type.

commands.title: Add graph node
commands.summary: Add or replace a graph node.
commands.description:
Adds a node to a graph or replaces it if the node id already exists. A node carries optional JSON properties, an optional declared object type (validated once the graph's ontology is frozen), and an optional entity binding that links the node to a row in another primitive. Cross-branch bindings are rejected.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_node_add
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to insert or upsert a node in a graph, optionally with JSON properties, a declared object type, or a binding to a KV/JSON/vector/event entity.

commands.title: Get graph node
commands.summary: Read one graph node.
commands.description:
Reads one node by id, returning its properties, declared object type, entity binding, and last-write commit coordinates. A removed or never-written node reads back as no data. Accepts `as_of` for time travel.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_graph_node_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to read a node's properties, declared type, or binding by node id. Returns null for a missing node.

commands.title: List graph nodes
commands.summary: List graph nodes.
commands.description:
Lists a graph's nodes in node-id order. Accepts an optional id prefix filter, an item limit (default 100), an exclusive cursor, and `as_of` for time travel. Each item carries the full node payload: properties, declared type, binding, and commit coordinates.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_graph_node_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to enumerate the nodes of a graph, optionally filtered by node-id prefix.

commands.title: Remove graph node
commands.summary: Remove a graph node and its edges.
commands.description:
Removes a node and every edge incident to it in one commit. Removing a node that does not exist is not an error: the acknowledgement reports `deleted: false`.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_node_remove
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to delete a node from a graph. Incident edges are removed with it.

commands.title: List graph nodes by type
commands.summary: List nodes declaring an object type.
commands.description:
Lists the nodes that declare a given object type, in node-id order. The type index is maintained from each node's declared `object_type`, so this works whether the ontology is draft or frozen. Accepts a limit, an exclusive cursor, and `as_of` for time travel.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_graph_nodes_by_type
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants every node of a given object type, for example all Person nodes. Works with draft or frozen ontologies.

commands.title: Define graph link type
commands.summary: Define a graph link type.
commands.description:
Declares a link type in the graph's ontology: a name, its source and target object types, an optional cardinality hint (for example `many-to-one`), and property definitions. Source and target must name declared object types by the time the ontology is frozen. After freezing, this command fails with `failed_precondition.engine.graph_ontology_frozen`.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_ontology_define_link_type
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to declare (or, while the ontology is still a draft, redefine) an edge link type between two object types, optionally with a cardinality hint and typed properties.

commands.title: Define graph object type
commands.summary: Define a graph object type.
commands.description:
Declares an object type in the graph's ontology: a name plus property definitions (`value_type`, `required`). While the ontology is a draft, redefining a type replaces it freely. After `graph.ontology.freeze`, the ontology is immutable and this command fails with `failed_precondition.engine.graph_ontology_frozen`.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_ontology_define_object_type
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to declare (or, while the ontology is still a draft, redefine) a node object type with named, typed properties in a graph's ontology.

commands.title: Delete graph link type
commands.summary: Delete a draft link type.
commands.description:
Removes a link type from the graph's draft ontology. Deleting a type that was never declared is not an error: the acknowledgement reports `deleted: false`. Once the ontology is frozen this command fails with `failed_precondition.engine.graph_ontology_frozen`.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_ontology_delete_link_type
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove a link type from a graph's draft ontology. Frozen ontologies cannot change.

commands.title: Delete graph object type
commands.summary: Delete a draft object type.
commands.description:
Removes an object type from the graph's draft ontology. Deleting a type that was never declared is not an error: the acknowledgement reports `deleted: false`. Once the ontology is frozen this command fails with `failed_precondition.engine.graph_ontology_frozen`.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_ontology_delete_object_type
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove an object type from a graph's draft ontology. Frozen ontologies cannot change.

commands.title: Freeze graph ontology
commands.summary: Freeze the graph ontology.
commands.description:
Validates the draft ontology and freezes it. Validation requires at least one declared type and rejects link types whose source or target reference undeclared object types (`failed_precondition.engine.graph_ontology_freeze`). After freezing, writes enforce declared node object types, required properties, and link-type endpoint rules; the ontology itself can no longer change (`failed_precondition.engine.graph_ontology_frozen`).

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_graph_ontology_freeze
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to lock a graph's ontology so subsequent node and edge writes are validated against the declared types. Freezing is permanent and validates the draft first.

commands.title: Read graph ontology
commands.summary: Read the graph ontology.
commands.description:
Reads the graph's ontology: its status (`draft` or `frozen`) plus every declared object type and link type with their property definitions. Returns no data before any type has been declared. Accepts `as_of` for time travel.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_graph_ontology_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to inspect a graph's ontology - its draft or frozen status and every declared object type and link type.

commands.title: Read graph ontology summary
commands.summary: Read the ontology with usage counts.
commands.description:
Reads the graph's ontology annotated with per-type usage: a node count for each object type and an edge count for each link type. Returns no data before any type has been declared. Accepts `as_of` for time travel.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_graph_ontology_summary
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants ontology types together with usage statistics - how many nodes declare each object type and how many edges use each link type.

commands.title: Sample graph nodes
commands.summary: Sample graph nodes.
commands.description:
Returns a deterministic representative sample of nodes from a graph: the total live node count and up to `count` nodes (default 10), evenly strided over the ordered node ids. Latest state only.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_graph_sample
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants a quick representative look at a graph's nodes without paging through all of them.

commands.title: Report model cache
commands.summary: Report loaded model cache state.
commands.description: Reports the runtime model cache as three lists of specs: the generation, embedding, and ranking models currently loaded in memory. Use it to check what is resident before generating, or to confirm that `inference unload` freed the models you expected. The lists reflect only in-memory engines, not models available on disk.

mcp.name: strata_inference_cache_status
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to see which inference models are currently loaded in memory.

commands.title: Report model capabilities
commands.summary: Report capabilities for a model spec.
commands.description: Parses a model spec into a provider and model name and reports what that combination supports without running the model. The result states whether generation, tokenization, embedding, and ranking are available, whether the operation requires network access or an API key, whether this binary was compiled with the provider feature needed to execute, whether the runtime currently permits network calls, and the known embedding dimension. Model specs are catalog names (`tinyllama`), catalog `name:quant` pairs (`tinyllama:q8_0`), local GGUF paths, or provider specs (`anthropic:claude-...`).

mcp.name: strata_inference_capability
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to know what a model spec can do — generate, tokenize, embed, or rank — and whether it needs the network, an API key, or a compiled provider feature.

commands.title: Detokenize token ids
commands.summary: Detokenize token ids with a local model.
commands.description: Decodes an ordered list of token ids back into text using a local model's vocabulary, returning the reconstructed string. Detokenization is a local-only operation: it requires a build with the local execution feature and returns `inference.unsupported_operation` for cloud provider specs.

mcp.name: strata_inference_detokenize
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to turn a local model's token ids back into text.

fixtures.replay_skip: detokenization loads a local model, which requires the local build feature

commands.title: Embed one or more texts
commands.summary: Embed one or more texts into vectors.
commands.description: Embeds text with an embedding-capable model and returns one vector per input, in order. The `input` field takes either a single string or an array of strings, so single and batch embedding share one command. The vector dimension is fixed by the model. Local embedding models require a build with the local execution feature; cloud embedding providers (OpenAI, Google) require the matching provider feature and an API key.

mcp.name: strata_inference_embed
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to turn text into embedding vectors with an inference model. The input accepts a single string or an array of strings.

fixtures.replay_skip: embedding runs a model: local execution needs the local build feature and cloud execution needs a provider API key

commands.title: Generate text
commands.summary: Generate text with an inference model.
commands.description: Runs a text-generation request against a local or cloud model and returns the completion, the reason generation stopped, and the provider-reported prompt and completion token counts. The request controls the maximum completion tokens, sampling temperature, top-k and top-p cutoffs, an optional deterministic seed, string and token-id stop sequences, and an optional GBNF grammar for constrained generation. Chat models expect their chat template already applied in the prompt. Local models require a build with the local execution feature; cloud providers require the matching provider feature and an API key.

mcp.name: strata_inference_generate
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants an inference model to produce or complete text from a prompt, with control over token budget, sampling, stop conditions, and grammar.

fixtures.replay_skip: text generation runs a model: local execution needs the local build feature and cloud execution needs a provider API key

commands.title: List catalog models
commands.summary: List catalog inference models.
commands.description: Lists every model in Strata's built-in catalog as a terminal page. Each entry reports the model's task (embed, generate, or rank), architecture, default quantization, embedding dimension, HuggingFace repository, approximate artifact size, and whether the model artifact is already present in the local model directory. Use `inference models local` to see only the downloaded models, or `inference models pull` to fetch one.

mcp.name: strata_inference_models_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to see which inference models Strata knows about, including their task, embedding dimension, and whether each is already downloaded.

fixtures.replay_skip: list marks each model is_local from the machine's model directory (STRATA_MODELS_DIR or ~/.strata/models), so the success output is machine-dependent

commands.title: List local models
commands.summary: List locally downloaded inference models.
commands.description: Lists the catalog models that have at least one quantization variant present in the local model directory, as a terminal page. Entries carry the same facts as `inference models list` but are restricted to models that can run without a further download. The local model directory is resolved from `STRATA_MODELS_DIR`, falling back to `~/.strata/models`.

mcp.name: strata_inference_models_local
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to know which inference models are already downloaded and ready to run offline.

fixtures.replay_skip: which models are present depends on the machine's model directory (STRATA_MODELS_DIR or ~/.strata/models)

commands.title: Download a model
commands.summary: Download an inference model locally.
commands.description: Resolves a catalog name or model spec and downloads the model artifact into the local model directory, returning the resolved local path. Honors `STRATA_MODELS_DIR` for the destination and `STRATA_HF_ENDPOINT` and `STRATA_HF_TOKEN` (or `HF_TOKEN`) for gated HuggingFace repositories. Downloading requires network access and a build with the local execution feature; cloud-only builds return `inference.unsupported_operation`.

mcp.name: strata_inference_models_pull
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to download or fetch an inference model so it can run locally, offline.

fixtures.replay_skip: model download performs network I/O and requires the local execution build feature

commands.title: Rank passages
commands.summary: Rank passages against a query.
commands.description: Scores each candidate passage against a query with a ranking model and returns one outcome per passage. Each item carries the passage's original index and either a relevance score or a per-item error with a stable code and a redacted message, so callers can reorder passages by score while keeping them tied to their inputs. Ranking is a local-only operation: it requires a build with the local execution feature and a ranking-capable model.

mcp.name: strata_inference_rank
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to score or reorder candidate passages by relevance to a query using a reranking model.

fixtures.replay_skip: ranking loads a local model, which requires the local build feature

commands.title: Tokenize text
commands.summary: Tokenize text with a local model.
commands.description: Encodes text into the token id sequence a local model would see and returns the ids in order. Set `add_special` to include the model's special tokens (such as beginning-of-sequence markers). Tokenization is a local-only operation: it requires a build with the local execution feature and returns `inference.unsupported_operation` for cloud provider specs.

mcp.name: strata_inference_tokenize
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to convert text into a local model's token ids, for example to measure prompt length or inspect tokenization.

fixtures.replay_skip: tokenization loads a local model, which requires the local build feature

commands.title: Unload cached models
commands.summary: Unload cached inference models.
commands.description: Removes cached model engines from the runtime to free memory. Pass a model spec to unload one entry, or omit it to unload every cached generation, embedding, and ranking model. The result reports whether any cached entry was actually removed. This affects only the in-memory runtime cache; it never deletes downloaded model files from disk.

mcp.name: strata_inference_unload
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to free memory by unloading a cached inference model, or all of them.

commands.title: Batch delete JSON values
commands.summary: Delete multiple JSON documents or paths in one itemwise batch.
commands.description:
Deletes multiple document/path entries and returns one positional mutation result per entry. Missing documents and paths are represented as no-op item results; applied items share one engine commit.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_json_batch_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove several JSON documents or nested fields together.

commands.title: Batch check JSON document existence
commands.summary: Check existence for multiple JSON documents.
commands.description:
Checks several JSON documents and returns positional boolean status values. The response preserves the input order.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_json_batch_exists
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to test whether several JSON documents exist.

commands.title: Batch get JSON values
commands.summary: Read multiple JSON values by document and path.
commands.description:
Reads several document/path entries and returns positional item results. Each item records whether the value was found and includes version metadata when present.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_json_batch_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to fetch several JSON documents or fields in one request.

commands.title: Batch set JSON values
commands.summary: Set multiple JSON values in one itemwise batch.
commands.description:
Writes multiple document/path/value entries using the executor batch contract. Valid items share one engine commit; entries targeting the same document are merged in order into a single new document version.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_json_batch_set
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to write several JSON documents or fields together in one commit.

commands.title: Count JSON documents
commands.summary: Count visible JSON documents.
commands.description:
Counts visible JSON documents in the selected branch and space, optionally constrained by a document key prefix.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_json_count
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants the number of visible JSON documents.

commands.title: Delete JSON value
commands.summary: Delete a whole JSON document or one path inside it.
commands.description:
Deletes the root path `$` to remove the whole document, or a nested path to remove one field or array element. Missing documents and paths produce a no-op delete acknowledgement rather than an error.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_json_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove a JSON document or delete one nested field from it.

commands.title: Check JSON document existence
commands.summary: Check whether one JSON document exists.
commands.description:
Returns a boolean status for one JSON document key without loading the stored document.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_json_exists
mcp.description: Use this when the user only needs to know if a JSON document is present.

commands.title: Get JSON value
commands.summary: Read the current or historical JSON value at a document path.
commands.description:
Reads the JSON value at a path inside a document. Current reads return the value with commit metadata; passing a timestamp returns the bare value visible at that point in time. A missing document or path is a found-false result, distinct from a stored JSON null.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_json_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to fetch a JSON document or one nested field by key and path.

commands.title: Read JSON document history
commands.summary: Read retained version history for one JSON document.
commands.description:
Returns retained full-document history rows for a JSON document, newest first, including delete tombstones. A document with no retained history maps to an optional-read result.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_json_history
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants prior versions, edits, or tombstones of a JSON document.

commands.title: Create JSON index
commands.summary: Create a JSON secondary index on a field path.
commands.description:
Creates a secondary index over one JSON field path with a numeric, tag, or text kind. Existing documents are indexed at creation and future writes maintain the index automatically. The current wire response is a transitional bare index definition.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_json_index_create
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to index a JSON field (numeric, tag, or text) for faster lookups.

commands.title: Drop JSON index
commands.summary: Drop a JSON secondary index by name.
commands.description:
Drops the named JSON secondary index and its stored entries. Documents are unaffected. The current wire response is a transitional boolean status.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_json_index_drop
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove an existing JSON field index.

commands.title: List JSON indexes
commands.summary: List JSON secondary indexes.
commands.description:
Lists JSON secondary index definitions in the selected branch and space, including each index's field path and kind.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_json_index_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to discover which JSON field indexes exist.

commands.title: List JSON document keys
commands.summary: List JSON document keys with optional prefix filtering.
commands.description:
Lists visible JSON document keys in byte order. Prefix, cursor, limit, and timestamp parameters constrain the page returned by the executor.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_json_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to page through JSON document keys.

commands.title: Sample JSON documents
commands.summary: Sample visible JSON documents.
commands.description:
Returns a bounded sample of visible JSON documents plus the total matching count. Useful for inspecting document shape before writing queries or indexes.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_json_sample
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants a quick look at stored JSON documents without paging through all of them.

commands.title: Scan JSON documents
commands.summary: Scan JSON documents with values and version facts.
commands.description:
Scans visible JSON documents starting at an optional document key. Each item includes the key, full document value, and commit metadata exposed by the executor output.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_json_scan
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to page through JSON document rows.

commands.title: Set JSON value
commands.summary: Set a JSON value at a document path, creating the document when missing.
commands.description:
Writes a JSON value at a path inside a document, creating the document and any missing intermediate objects when needed. Setting the root path `$` replaces the whole document; setting a nested path like `$.profile.name` updates one field and records a new document version.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_json_set
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to write, update, or upsert JSON data — a whole document (path `$`) or one nested field.

commands.title: Batch delete KV values
commands.summary: Delete multiple KV keys in one itemwise batch.
commands.description:
Deletes multiple KV keys and returns one positional mutation result per key. Missing keys are represented as no-op item results.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_kv_batch_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove several KV keys.

commands.title: Batch check KV existence
commands.summary: Check existence for multiple KV keys.
commands.description:
Checks several KV keys and returns positional boolean status values. The response preserves the input order.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_kv_batch_exists
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to test whether several KV keys exist.

commands.title: Batch get KV values
commands.summary: Read multiple KV values by key.
commands.description:
Reads several KV keys and returns positional item results. Each item records whether the corresponding key was found and includes value metadata when present.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_kv_batch_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to fetch several KV keys in one request.

commands.title: Batch put KV values
commands.summary: Store multiple KV values in one itemwise batch.
commands.description:
Writes multiple KV entries using the executor batch contract. Valid items share commit facts where the underlying engine applies them together.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_kv_batch_put
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to write several KV keys together.

commands.title: Count KV keys
commands.summary: Count visible KV keys.
commands.description:
Counts visible KV keys in the selected branch and space, optionally constrained by a key prefix.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_kv_count
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants the number of visible KV keys.

commands.title: Delete KV value
commands.summary: Delete one visible KV key.
commands.description:
Deletes the current visible value for a KV key. Missing keys produce a no-op delete acknowledgement rather than a read-style missing value.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_kv_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove a KV value by key.

commands.title: Check KV existence
commands.summary: Check whether one KV key exists.
commands.description:
Returns a boolean status for one KV key without loading the stored value.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_kv_exists
mcp.description: Use this when the user only needs to know if a KV key is present.

commands.title: Get KV value
commands.summary: Read the current or historical value for one KV key.
commands.description:
Reads one KV key from the selected branch and space. The optional timestamp reads the value visible at that point in time when history is retained.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_kv_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to fetch a binary KV value by key.

commands.title: Read KV history
commands.summary: Read retained version history for one KV key.
commands.description:
Returns retained history rows for a KV key when history is available. Missing or unavailable history maps to an optional-read result.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_kv_history
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants prior values and tombstones for a KV key.

commands.title: List KV keys
commands.summary: List KV keys with optional prefix filtering.
commands.description:
Lists visible KV keys in byte order. Prefix, cursor, limit, and timestamp parameters constrain the page returned by the executor.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_kv_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to page through KV keys.

commands.title: Put KV value
commands.summary: Store or replace a KV value by key.
commands.description:
Writes a binary value to the selected KV space. If the key already exists, Strata replaces the visible value and records a new version.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_kv_put
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to write, overwrite, or upsert a binary KV value.

commands.title: Sample KV rows
commands.summary: Sample visible KV rows.
commands.description:
Returns a deterministic bounded sample of visible KV rows plus the total matching count.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_kv_sample
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants a compact sample of KV rows.

commands.title: Scan KV rows
commands.summary: Scan KV rows with values and version facts.
commands.description:
Scans visible KV rows starting at an optional key. Each item includes the key, value, and commit metadata exposed by the executor output.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_kv_scan
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to page through KV key-value rows.

commands.title: Create product space
commands.summary: Create a product space on a branch.
commands.description:
Creates a product space in the branch catalog. Creation is idempotent: creating a space that already exists succeeds with `created: false` and no mutation effect. Names reserved for engine control data are rejected with `invalid_argument.engine.product_space_reserved`.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_space_create
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants a new named data namespace (space) on a branch.

commands.title: Delete product space
commands.summary: Delete a product space from a branch.
commands.description:
Drops the product space from the branch catalog. The `default` space refuses deletion with `invalid_argument.engine.space_delete_default`. A space that still contains visible data refuses deletion with `failed_precondition.engine.space_not_empty` unless `force: true` is set, which tombstones the visible rows first and reports the count. Deleting a space that does not exist succeeds with `deleted: false`.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_space_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove a space, optionally force-deleting its data.

commands.title: Check product space existence
commands.summary: Check whether a product space exists on a branch.
commands.description:
Reports whether the named product space is cataloged on the target branch. A missing space is `false`, not an error; reserved names are rejected with `invalid_argument.engine.product_space_reserved`.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_space_exists
mcp.description: Use this when the user asks whether a specific space exists.

commands.title: List product spaces
commands.summary: List product spaces on a branch.
commands.description:
Lists the product space names cataloged on the target branch as a terminal page. Every branch has a `default` space; additional spaces isolate data namespaces within the same branch.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_space_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to see which product spaces exist on a branch.

commands.title: Batch delete vectors
commands.summary: Delete multiple vectors by key.
commands.description:
Deletes multiple vector keys and returns one positional mutation result per input key.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_vector_batch_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove several vector keys.

commands.title: Batch check vector existence
commands.summary: Check existence for multiple vector keys.
commands.description:
Checks several vector keys in one collection and returns positional boolean status values. The response preserves the input order.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_vector_batch_exists
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to test whether several vector keys exist in a collection.

commands.title: Batch get vectors
commands.summary: Read multiple vectors by key.
commands.description:
Reads several vector keys and returns positional item results. Each item records found or missing state.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_vector_batch_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to fetch several vectors in one request.

commands.title: Batch upsert vectors
commands.summary: Upsert multiple vectors in one itemwise batch.
commands.description:
Writes multiple vector entries and returns positional mutation results. Valid items share commit facts where the engine applies them together.

Itemwise batches return one positional item result per input item. The outer batch status summarizes whether all, some, or none of the items succeeded.

mcp.name: strata_vector_batch_upsert
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to write several vector embeddings.

commands.title: Create vector collection
commands.summary: Create a vector collection with a dimension and metric.
commands.description:
Creates a collection for dense vectors. The dimension and metric become part of the collection contract for future upserts and queries.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_collection_create
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to add a new vector collection.

commands.title: Delete vector collection
commands.summary: Delete a vector collection.
commands.description:
Deletes the selected vector collection from the current branch and space. The current wire response is a transitional boolean status.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_collection_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove an existing vector collection.

commands.title: List vector collections
commands.summary: List vector collections.
commands.description:
Lists vector collections visible in the selected branch and space, including collection dimension, metric, and count facts.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_vector_collection_list
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to discover vector collections.

commands.title: Read vector collection stats
commands.summary: Read facts for one vector collection.
commands.description:
Reads collection-level facts for one vector collection. The current wire response uses the collection-list output with one item.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_collection_stats
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants collection dimension, metric, and count information.

commands.title: Count vectors
commands.summary: Count visible vectors in a collection.
commands.description:
Counts vectors visible in the selected collection, branch, and space.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_count
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants the size of a vector collection.

commands.title: Delete vector
commands.summary: Delete one vector key.
commands.description:
Deletes one visible vector entry from a collection. Missing keys are represented as no-op mutation acknowledgements.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_delete
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove a vector by key.

commands.title: Delete all vectors
commands.summary: Delete all vectors in a collection.
commands.description:
Deletes all vectors visible in the selected collection while preserving the collection itself.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_delete_all
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to clear a vector collection.

commands.title: Delete vectors by filter
commands.summary: Delete vectors matching a metadata filter.
commands.description:
Scans the collection for visible vectors matching the metadata filter and deletes the matching rows as a bulk mutation.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_delete_by_filter
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to remove vectors selected by metadata.

commands.title: Check vector existence
commands.summary: Check whether one vector key exists.
commands.description:
Returns a boolean status for one vector key without loading the embedding.

Status commands return a scalar or compact status payload and do not mutate database state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_exists
mcp.description: Use this when the user only needs to know if a vector key is present.

commands.title: Get vector
commands.summary: Read one vector by key.
commands.description:
Reads one visible vector entry. The optional timestamp reads the vector visible at that point in time when retained history allows it.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_vector_get
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants an embedding and metadata for one vector key.

commands.title: Read vector history
commands.summary: Read retained vector history for one key.
commands.description:
Returns retained history rows for one vector key, including vector revision facts and tombstones when present.

Optional reads distinguish present data from missing data. When version or timestamp facts exist on the executor output, SDK mappings should preserve them.

mcp.name: strata_vector_history
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants prior vector revisions and tombstones.

commands.title: Query vector index
commands.summary: Search vectors and return index diagnostics.
commands.description:
Runs vector search and includes planner diagnostics such as index policy, source usage, artifact status, and fallback facts.

Search responses return a bounded list of matches ordered by the engine. They are not cursor pages unless a later command explicitly advertises pagination.

Diagnostic responses include operational facts intended for debugging and tuning. They should not be required for application correctness.

mcp.name: strata_vector_index_query
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants vector matches plus index planner diagnostics.

commands.title: List vector keys
commands.summary: List vector keys in a collection.
commands.description:
Lists visible vector keys with optional prefix and cursor arguments.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_vector_keys
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to page through vector keys.

commands.title: Update vector metadata
commands.summary: Patch metadata for one vector.
commands.description:
Applies a top-level metadata patch to one visible vector. Missing vectors return a no-op mutation acknowledgement.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_metadata_update
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to update vector metadata without replacing the embedding.

commands.title: Query vectors
commands.summary: Search a vector collection.
commands.description:
Runs vector search through the engine planner and returns the best matches with scores and optional metadata.

Search responses return a bounded list of matches ordered by the engine. They are not cursor pages unless a later command explicitly advertises pagination.

mcp.name: strata_vector_query
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants nearest-neighbor vector matches.

commands.title: Sample vectors
commands.summary: Sample vectors with values and version facts.
commands.description:
Returns a deterministic representative sample of vectors from a collection: the total live count and up to `count` entries (default 10), evenly strided over the ordered keys. Latest state only.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_vector_sample
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants a quick representative look at stored vectors without paging through the whole collection.

commands.title: Scan vectors
commands.summary: Scan vectors with values and version facts.
commands.description:
Scans visible vectors in a collection starting at an optional key. Each item includes the key, embedding, metadata, and commit metadata exposed by the executor output.

Paginated responses use opaque cursors. Clients should pass the returned cursor back to the same command shape and must not parse cursor contents.

mcp.name: strata_vector_scan
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to page through vector rows.

commands.title: Upsert vector
commands.summary: Insert or replace one vector.
commands.description:
Upserts one vector key with a dense embedding and optional metadata. The vector dimension must match the collection configuration.

Successful mutations return an acknowledgement that identifies the affected target, the mutation effect, and commit facts when the operation changed stored state.

mcp.name: strata_vector_upsert
mcp.description: Use this when the user wants to store a vector embedding and optional metadata.
